zh.Battle of Halidon Hill, Berwick-upon-Twe: 英國諾圖波藍德貝維其可-朋-特維德
zh.lifeSummary: 當Simon Fraser of Brotherton and 1st of Lovat, Sheriff of Kincardine於大約1280年出生時，他的父親Sir Andrew Fraser of Touchfraser42歲，母親Beatrix Cheyne41歲。他和Margaret Sinclair至少有5個兒子和1個女兒。他去世於1333年7月19日在英國諾圖波藍德貝維其可-朋-特維德享年54歲。

nameMeanings.name: Fraser
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: apparently a nickname from Anglo-Norman French fraser(e), fresere ‘strawberry plant’. Fresel and Frisel, from Anglo-Norman French fresel ‘strawberry’, are early variants of the Fraser surname, the modern Gaelicized form of which is Friseal. See Frizzell . The crest on the Scottish family's coat of arms takes the form of a strawberry plant, but its antiquity is unknown. Claims of a habitational derivation, in particular from a place called la Frézelière in Anjou (France), are attractive but they lack verifiable evidence.
Americanized form of one or more similar (like-sounding) Jewish surnames.

History: There are two clans Fraser in Scotland, with common ancestry going back to Simon Fraser of Keith in East Lothian, who lived in the 12th century. One of these clans has its seat at Philorth Castle (subsequently re-named Cairnbulg) on the northeast coast of Scotland. Sir Alexander Fraser, 8th laird of Philorth (c. 1536–1623) converted the fishing village of Faithlie into the burgh of Fraserburgh in the 1590s. The other clan is Fraser of Lovat, associated mainly with the city of Inverness are. They are descended from Simon Fraser, a younger son, who lived in the early 14th century. In Gaelic, the head of Clan Fraser of Lovat is known as Mac Shimi ‘son of Simon’.

variants.name: Frashier

variants.name: Frizzell
meanings: English and Irish: nickname from Old French fresel, frisel ‘lace, ribbon, braid, brooch, frill, ruff’, for someone with an ornate style of dress, or ‘object of little value’ (a diminutive of frese, of ancient Germanic origin).
Scottish: from Anglo-Norman French fresel ‘strawberry’, an alternative form of Fraser .

variants.name: Frazier
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Fraser  .

variants.name: Frasher
meanings: Scottish and northern Irish: variant of   Fraser  .
Possibly an altered form of German Fratzscher (see Frutchey ).

nameMeanings.name: Simon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Usual English form of Simeon , borne in the New Testament by various characters: two apostles, a brother of Jesus, a Pharisee, a leper, a tanner, a sorcerer (who offered money for the gifts of the Holy Ghost, giving rise to the term simony), and the man who carried Jesus's cross to the Crucifixion.

variants.name: Simen
meanings: Usual English form of Simeon , borne in the New Testament by various characters: two apostles, a brother of Jesus, a Pharisee, a leper, a tanner, a sorcerer (who offered money for the gifts of the Holy Ghost, giving rise to the term simony), and the man who carried Jesus's cross to the Crucifixion.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

en.lifeSummary: When Simon Fraser of Brotherton and 1st of Lovat, Sheriff of Kincardine was born about 1280, his father, Sir Andrew Fraser of Touchfraser, was 42 and his mother, Beatrix Cheyne, was 41. He had at least 5 sons and 1 daughter with Margaret Sinclair. He died on 19 July 1333, in Berwick-upon-Tweed, Northumberland, England, at the age of 54.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Simon Fraser of Brotherton and 1st of Lovat, Sheriff of Kincardine est né environ 1280, son père, Sir Andrew Fraser of Touchfraser, avait 42 ans et sa mère, Beatrix Cheyne, avait 41 ans. Il a eu au moins 5 fils et 1 fille avec Margaret Sinclair. Il est décédé le 19 juillet 1333, à Berwick-upon-Tweed, Northumberland, Angleterre, à l’âge de 54 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Simon Fraser of Brotherton and 1st of Lovat, Sheriff of Kincardine um 1280 geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir Andrew Fraser of Touchfraser 42 und seine Mutter Beatrix Cheyne 41. Er hatte mit Margaret Sinclair mindestens 5 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 19. Juli 1333 im Alter von 54 Jahren in Berwick-upon-Tweed, Northumberland, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Simon Fraser of Brotherton and 1st of Lovat, Sheriff of Kincardine è nato il circa 1280, il suo padre, Sir Andrew Fraser of Touchfraser, aveva 42 anni e il suo madre, Beatrix Cheyne, aveva 41 anni. Lui avuto almeno 5 figli e 1 figlia con Margaret Sinclair. Morì il 19 luglio 1333, nel Berwick-upon-Tweed, Northumberland, England, all'età di 54 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Simon Fraser of Brotherton and 1st of Lovat, Sheriff of Kincardineが約1280年に生まれた時，父Sir Andrew Fraser of Touchfraserは42歳，母Beatrix Cheyneは41歳であった。彼はMargaret Sinclairとの間に少なくとも5人の息子と1人の娘をもうけた。彼は1333年7月19日にイギリスノースウンバーランドバーウィク-ウプオン-トウィードで54歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Simon Fraser of Brotherton and 1st of Lovat, Sheriff of Kincardine(이)가 대략 1280년에 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Andrew Fraser of Touchfraser(은)는 42세였고, 그의 어머니 Beatrix Cheyne(은)는 41세였다. 그는 Margaret Sinclair(와)과 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1333년 7월 19일 영국 노르스벌랜드 버윅-업-트위드에서 54세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Simon Fraser of Brotherton and 1st of Lovat, Sheriff of Kincardine nasceu aproximadamente 1280, seu pai, Sir Andrew Fraser of Touchfraser, tinha 42 anos e sua mãe, Beatrix Cheyne, tinha 41 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 5 filhos e 1 filha com Margaret Sinclair. Ele faleceu em 19 de julho de 1333, em Berwick-upon-Tweed, Northumberland, Inglaterra, com 54 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Simon Fraser of Brotherton and 1st of Lovat, Sheriff of Kincardine родился около 1280 г., его отцу по имени Sir Andrew Fraser of Touchfraser было 42 года, а матери, которую звали Beatrix Cheyne, 41. У него было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 1 дочь от Margaret Sinclair. Он умер 19 июля 1333 г. в местности бирвикк-упон-твид, нортумбирланд, Англия в возрасте 54 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Simon Fraser of Brotherton and 1st of Lovat, Sheriff of Kincardine nació aproximadamente 1280, su papá, Sir Andrew Fraser of Touchfraser, tenía 42 años y su mamá, Beatrix Cheyne, tenía 41 años. Tuvo por lo menos 5 hijos y 1 hija con Margaret Sinclair. Murió el 19 de julio de 1333, en Berwick-upon-Tweed, Northumberland, Inglaterra, a la edad de 54 años.
